w:r.w:t: Core Java | JavaFX | MySQL | JDBC | Maven

Campus Management System (CMS) is a desktop-based academic administration application built entirely in Core Java. It manages students, faculty, courses, departments, and enrollments while demonstrating fundamental software engineering concepts — OOP, JDBC, multithreading, socket-based networking, and RMI — without relying on any heavy frameworks.
Provide role-based access for Admin, Faculty, and Student users
Support full CRUD operations for all academic entities
Enforce strict data validation using regex at every input point
Persist all data using JDBC with MySQL as the backend database
Implement real-time chat between users via Java Sockets
Expose grade and attendance data through RMI remote services
Demonstrate clean layered architecture: model → dao → service → ui
Domain POJOs — User (abstract), Student, Faculty, Admin, Course, Department, Enrollment
JDBC Data Access Objects — one per entity (StudentDAO, FacultyDAO, etc.)
Business logic layer — validation, orchestration, error handling
JavaFX controllers and FXML screens — one controller per view
Lightweight HTTP endpoint for future REST integration
The project follows a strict 4-layer architecture. Each layer depends only on the layer directly below it, ensuring clean separation of concerns and testability.
JavaFX controllers receive user input, call services, and update the view. No business logic here.
Validates inputs, applies business rules, calls DAO methods, throws custom exceptions.
Executes parameterised SQL via JDBC PreparedStatements. Maps ResultSets to model objects.
Pure Java POJOs with field-level validation in setters. No DB or UI dependencies.

The User class is abstract and serves as the root of all user types. Concrete subclasses add role-specific fields. This mirrors the database design where the users table holds common credentials and per-role tables extend it.
User — userId, email, passwordHash, role, createdAt. Abstract methods: getDisplayName(), getRole()
Student — studentId, rollNumber, deptId, firstName, lastName, phone, academicYear, dateOfBirth
Faculty — facultyId, employeeId, deptId, firstName, lastName, designation, phone
Internal IDs (INT AUTO_INCREMENT) are used for all DB joins and foreign keys
User-visible identifiers (rollNumber, employeeId) are UNIQUE-constrained but never used as FK
All setters perform regex validation and throw IllegalArgumentException on failure
DBConnection uses the Singleton pattern — one connection pool for the entire app
DAOs use PreparedStatement exclusively — no string-concatenated SQL (prevents SQL injection)
Services throw custom checked exceptions (e.g. DuplicateRollNumberException) for precise error handling in the UI

The database contains 7 tables. All tables use INT AUTO_INCREMENT primary keys. The users table is the anchor for authentication; the per-role tables (students, faculty, admins) join to it via user_id.
Authentication & role assignment for all user types
Academic departments — CS, IT, MBA, etc.
Student profile and academic year tracking
Faculty profile with designation and dept
enrollments (student_id, course_id) — UNIQUE composite key (no duplicate enrollments)
users → students/faculty/admins: ON DELETE CASCADE (deleting a user removes their profile)
courses.faculty_id: ON DELETE SET NULL (deleting a faculty member un-assigns their courses)
Base class: userId, email, role. Abstract: getDisplayName(), getRole()
rollNumber, deptId, firstName, lastName, phone, academicYear, dob
employeeId, deptId, firstName, lastName, designation, phone
courseId, deptId, facultyId, courseCode, courseName, credits, semester
enrollmentId, studentId, courseId, enrolledOn, status enum
insert(), update(), delete(), findById(), findByRollNo(), findAll(), findByDept()
insert(), update(), delete(), findById(), findByEmpId(), findAll(), findByDept()
insert(), update(), delete(), findById(), findByCode(), findByDept(), findAll()
insert(), update(), delete(), findById(), findByCode(), findAll()
addStudent(), updateStudent(), deleteStudent(), getStudent(), listAll() — validates before DAO call
login() — verifies email + password, returns typed User object
getConnection() — single JDBC connection, reads config.properties
Static regex methods: isValidEmail(), isValidPhone(), isValidRollNo(), isValidEmpId(), isValidCourseCode()
ServerSocket on port 9090, spawns ClientHandler thread per connection
Connects to ChatServer, sends/receives messages via Socket streams
Concrete RMI implementation backed by DAO calls
Basic HTTP server endpoint (future REST integration hook)
JavaFX entry point — launches the app, loads Login screen
Email + password form, role detection, navigates to dashboard
Navigation hub for Admin — links to all management screens
Course CRUD, assign faculty to course
Enroll/drop student from courses, view enrolled list
Student home — view profile, enrolled courses
Faculty home — view courses, enrolled students
Chat UI — text area + send button, backed by ChatClient
Email + password login with role detection (Admin / Faculty / Student)
Password stored as SHA-256 hash — never plain text
Failed login shows descriptive error (wrong password vs user not found)
Successful login redirects to role-appropriate dashboard
Session held in memory for the application lifetime
Add student: roll number, name, dept, academic year, DOB, email, phone — all validated
Edit student: update any field except roll number (immutable after creation)
Delete student: cascades to enrollments and user account
Search by roll number, name, or department
View all students in a sortable/filterable TableView
Add faculty: employee ID, name, dept, designation, email, phone
Edit/delete faculty — unlinks from courses on delete (SET NULL)
Add course: code, name, credits, semester, department, assign faculty
Edit/delete courses — handles enrolled student impact gracefully
View all courses with assigned faculty name
Full CRUD for departments — prevent deletion if students/faculty/courses linked
Enroll a student in a course (admin override)
Drop a student from a course
View all enrollments filtered by student or course
Real-time chat with peers or faculty via ChatClient
View assigned courses and enrolled student lists
ChatServer runs on port 9090, accepts multiple clients via threads
Each connected client identified by userId and role
Messages broadcast to selected recipient or all connected users
ChatController in JavaFX binds to ChatClient for live message display
GradeServiceImpl bound to RMI registry on port 1099
Can be invoked from a separate process or remote machine
The project is split into 6 phases. Each phase is fully functional and testable before moving to the next. This bottom-up approach ensures every layer is solid before depending on it.
pom.xml, config.properties, DBConnection, Validator, LoggerUtil, SQL schema
User (abstract), Student, Faculty, Admin, Course, Department, Enrollment — with validation
All 7 DAOs with JDBC PreparedStatements, tested against live DB
All 6 services, custom exceptions, business rule enforcement, JUnit tests
Login screen, Admin/Student/Faculty dashboards, all CRUD screens, Chat UI
ChatServer/Client, GradeServiceImpl RMI, logging, final testing, README update
Create pom.xml with JavaFX, MySQL Connector/J, JUnit 5 dependencies
Write config.properties with JDBC URL, username, password
Write Validator.java with all static regex methods
Run the SQL schema script to create campusdb
Write abstract User class with common fields and abstract methods
Write Student extends User — all fields with validated setters
Write Course, Department, Enrollment as standalone POJOs
Unit test every setter with valid and invalid inputs
Write DBConnection tests — verify live connection to MySQL
Write StudentDAO — insert, update, delete, findById, findByRollNo, findAll
Write FacultyDAO, AdminDAO, CourseDAO, DepartmentDAO in the same pattern
Write EnrollmentDAO — enroll, drop, isEnrolled, findByStudent
Write StudentDAOTest with JUnit 5 (create → read → update → delete cycle)
Write StudentService — wraps DAO calls with validation + exception handling
Write AuthService — login logic with password hash comparison
Configure MainFrame.java as JavaFX Application entry point
Build Chat screen backed by ChatClient
Implement ChatClient and wire to ChatController
Implement GradeServiceImpl and bind to RMI registry
End-to-end test all flows as Admin, Student, Faculty
Update README with setup and run instructions
DAOs throw SQLException — caught and wrapped as RuntimeException or custom exception in service
Services throw custom checked exceptions for known business rule violations
UI controllers catch service exceptions and show user-friendly Alert dialogs
All exceptions logged via LoggerUtil before being re-thrown or handled
ALL SQL uses PreparedStatement — zero string concatenation in queries
Sensitive data (password) hashed with SHA-256 before storage
Transactions used for multi-step operations (e.g. insert user then insert student)
All DB/service calls happen off the JavaFX Application Thread using Task<> or Platform.runLater()
FXML files co-located with their controller class in ui/
No business logic in controllers — they only call services
Code each file in this exact sequence. Each file is a dependency for those below it.
Replace plain JDBC with JPA/Hibernate for ORM-based persistence
Add REST API layer using Spring Boot for a web frontend
Password hashing with BCrypt instead of SHA-256
CSV/PDF report export for attendance and grades
Analytics dashboard with charts (JFreeChart or JavaFX Charts)
Docker containerisation of the MySQL instance
CI/CD pipeline with GitHub Actions running mvn test on every push
Java SE 17 | JavaFX | MySQL | JDBC | Maven | Sockets | RMI

Designed and built a desktop-based campus management system from scratch using Core Java and JDBC to manage students, faculty, courses, departments, and enrollments. Implemented a role-based user hierarchy (Admin, Faculty, Student) using abstract class inheritance and polymorphism. Enforced field-level data validation using regex across all model setters. Applied a 4-layer architecture (model / dao / service / ui) with clean separation of concerns. Added real-time multi-user chat using Java Sockets with multithreaded client handling, and exposed grade retrieval as a remote service via Java RMI. Used JavaFX with FXML for the desktop UI.
Campus Management System — Project Plan | Pranav Dadhe | github.com/pranavdadhe1806
